title: Getting Started with overlay
text: overlay provides tools render ggplot2 gt objects code snippets images, apply perspective distortions, composite background images create “Heads-Display” (HUD) effect.

title: Getting Started with overlay
text: core function hud_overlay(), handles rendering, optional borders, perspective warps, compositing one go.

title: Getting Started with overlay
text: can use ggplot2 figure, gt table, code snippet.
code: library(overlay) library(ggplot2) library(gt) library(tibble) library(magick)  # A gt table weather_tbl <- tibble(   icon     = "snowflake",   location = "**King George Island, Antarctica**",   temp     = "-2 °C",   wind     = "37 km/h",   precip   = "10%" ) |>   gt() |>   fmt_markdown(columns = location) |>   tab_header(title = md("**Weather**"), subtitle = "Monday, April 06, 2026") |>   opt_stylize(style = 6, color = "blue")  # A ggplot2 plot chinstrap <- penguins |> subset(species == "Chinstrap")  penguin_plot <- ggplot(chinstrap, aes(x = flipper_len, y = body_mass)) +   geom_point(color = "#5b9bd5", alpha = 0.7, size = 2) +   labs(title = "Chinstrap Penguins", x = "Flipper length (mm)", y = "Body mass (g)") +   theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +   theme(     panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),     plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA)   )

title: Getting Started with overlay
text: Use hud_overlay() place objects onto background image.
code: # Path to a background image bg <- system.file("extdata", "penguins.jpg", package = "overlay")  result <- weather_tbl |>   hud_overlay(     background = bg,     placement  = "left",     size       = "large",     panel      = TRUE,     opacity    = 0.85   ) |>   hud_overlay(     overlay    = penguin_plot,     background = _,               placement  = "right",     size       = "large",     tilt       = "right",     panel      = TRUE,     opacity    = 0.9   )  # View the result result

title: Getting Started with overlay
text: show code overlay, can create syntax-highlighted code images using carbon_image(), uses carbonara API render code carbon.js styling.

title: Getting Started with overlay
code: # Create a code snippet code <- ' ggplot(penguins, aes(bill_len, island)) +   geom_point(aes(color = species)) +   theme_minimal()'  # Generate code image code_img <- carbon_image(code, lang = "r", theme = "dark")  # Overlay on a background bg <- system.file("extdata", "penguins.jpg", package = "overlay")  result <- hud_overlay(   overlay    = code_img,   background = bg,   placement  = "center",   size       = "xl" )

title: Getting Started with overlay
text: potential use case showing visualization alongside code created :
code: # Create a plot p <- ggplot(chinstrap, aes(x = flipper_len, y = body_mass)) +   geom_point(color = "#5b9bd5", alpha = 0.7, size = 2) +   labs(title = "Chinstrap Penguins") +   theme_minimal()  # Generate the corresponding code image plot_code <- 'ggplot(chinstrap, aes(x = flipper_len, y = body_mass)) +   geom_point(color = "#5b9bd5", alpha = 0.7, size = 2) +   labs(title = "Chinstrap Penguins") +   theme_minimal()'  code_img <- carbon_image(plot_code, lang = "r", theme = "dark")  # Combine both using the pipe placeholder plotandcode <- hud_overlay(   overlay    = p,   background = bg,   placement  = "left",   size       = "xl",   tilt       = "left",   opacity    = 0.85 ) |>   hud_overlay(     overlay    = code_img,     background = _,          # Pipe placeholder     placement  = "right",     size       = "xl",     opacity    = 1   )

title: Getting Started with overlay
text: carbon_image() supports various customization options:
code: # Light theme code_img_light <- carbon_image(   code,   lang = "r",   theme = "light" )  # Custom font and size code_img_custom <- carbon_image(   code,   lang = "r",   theme = "dark",   fontSize = "16px",   fontFamily = "Fira Code" )  # Python code python_code <- 'import pandas as pd import matplotlib.pyplot as plt  df = pd.read_csv("data.csv") df.plot(x="date", y="value")'  python_img <- carbon_image(python_code, lang = "python", theme = "dark")

title: Getting Started with overlay
text: hud_panel() adds dark frame optional border. can pass list arguments panel hud_overlay() customization.
code: result_custom <- weather_tbl |>   hud_overlay(     background   = bg,     placement    = "left",     size         = "large",     panel        = list(       panel_color  = "#0D3B1ACC",       border_color = "#39FF14DD",       border_width = 10,       padding      = 30     )   )

title: Getting Started with overlay
text: tilt argument provides easy presets ("left", "right", "top", "bottom"), can specify corners manually precise control. corner specified c(dx, dy) offset natural position.
code: p_warped <- penguin_plot |>   hud_overlay(     background = bg,     corners = list(       tl = c(-50, -20),        tr = c(20, 10),       bl = c(-30, 0),       br = c(10, -10)     )   )

title: Getting Started with overlay
text: Since hud_overlay() returns magick-image, can chain multiple calls using base pipe placeholder _ (R ≥ 4.2.0):
code: # Multiple overlays on the same background multi_overlay <- weather_tbl |>   hud_overlay(     background = bg,     placement  = "top-left",     size       = "medium",     panel      = TRUE   ) |>   hud_overlay(     overlay    = penguin_plot,     background = _,     placement  = "bottom-right",     size       = "large",     tilt       = "right"   ) |>   hud_overlay(     overlay    = code_img,     background = _,     placement  = "bottom-left",     size       = "medium"   )

title: Authors and Citation
text: Luis D. Verde Arregoitia. Author, maintainer, copyright holder.

what: Render and Overlay ggplot2 and gt objects or Code Snippets with Perspective and Transparency
title: Render and Overlay ggplot2 and gt objects or Code Snippets with Perspective and Transparency
text: overlay renders ggplot2 gt objects well code snippets magick images, optionally adds heads-display (HUD) panel, applies perspective warps, composites result background image optional transparency. done quite easily PowerPoint graphic design software, writing package lets us work objects R environment programmatically reproducibly. Example outputs. Left: gt table added border; Right: ggplot2 object perspective tilt underlying code carbon.js image. Photograph Derek Oyen, Unsplash.

title: Render and Overlay ggplot2 and gt objects or Code Snippets with Perspective and Transparency
text: Install overlay GitHub remotes (pak) package uses magick (ImageMagick). Ensure ImageMagick installed system (see System requirements ).
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("luisdva/overlay")  # Alternatively, with pak # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("luisdva/overlay")

title: Render and Overlay ggplot2 and gt objects or Code Snippets with Perspective and Transparency
text: package five functions can used individually chained together:

title: Render and Overlay ggplot2 and gt objects or Code Snippets with Perspective and Transparency
code: library(ggplot2) library(overlay)  p <- ggplot(na.omit(penguins), aes(bill_len, body_mass)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +   theme_minimal()  bg <- system.file("extdata", "penguins.jpg", package = "overlay")  out <- p |>   hud_overlay(     background = bg,      placement  = "bottom-right",     size       = "medium",     panel      = TRUE,     tilt       = "left",     opacity    = 0.9   )  # View or save # magick::image_browse(out)

title: Render and Overlay ggplot2 and gt objects or Code Snippets with Perspective and Transparency
text: hud_overlay() wraps relevant functions (render → panel → warp → overlay) single call. object overlay first argument ggplot2 gt objects can piped directly function. Precedence: corners > tilt; explicit width/height > size; explicit x/y > placement. tilt convenience preset. full control, supply corners directly — entry c(dx, dy) pixel offset corner’s natural position:
code: hud_overlay(   overlay,           # ggplot2 or gt object, or carbon image of a code snippet   background,        # path, URL, or magick-image   placement = NULL,  # "left", "right", "centre", "top", "bottom", "top-left", …   x = NULL,          # pixel offset (overrides placement)   y = NULL,   margin = 40L,      # edge gap when using placement   size = NULL,       # "small", "medium", "large", "xl", "xxl"   width = 400,       # explicit pixel dimensions (overrides size)   height = 300,   bg = "transparent",# background color for the rendered overlay   res = 150,         # DPI resolution for rendering   panel = FALSE,     # TRUE, FALSE, or named list forwarded to hud_panel()   tilt = NULL,       # "none", "left", "right", "top", "bottom"   corners = NULL,    # named list of c(dx, dy) offsets: tl / tr / bl / br   keep_size = TRUE,  # crop/pad warped result back to original size   opacity = 0.85,   supersample = 2L,  # anti-aliasing factor; increase for steep warps in case of visual artifacts   operator = "over"  # ImageMagick operator for overlays ) # Lean the panel to the left p |>   hud_overlay(     background = system.file("extdata", "penguins.jpg", package = "overlay"),     corners = list(tl = c(-110, 0), bl = c(-110, 0))   )

title: Render and Overlay ggplot2 and gt objects or Code Snippets with Perspective and Transparency
text: Since hud_overlay() returns magick-image object, can chain calls add several overlays background. Use base pipe placeholder _ pass result one overlay background next: _ placeholder (available R ≥ 4.2.0) passes output first hud_overlay() call background argument second call. lets us build complex compositions multiple plots, tables, code snippets single background image. Note tilt placement options overlay independent inherited previous steps.
code: library(ggplot2) library(overlay)  # Create a plot penguin_plot <- ggplot(penguins, aes(x = species, fill = species)) +   geom_bar() +   theme_minimal() +   labs(title = "Penguin Species Count")  # Create code image code <- 'ggplot(penguins, aes(x = species, fill = species)) +   geom_bar() +   theme_minimal() +   labs(title = "Penguin Species Count")'  code_img <- carbon_image(code, lang = "r", theme = "dark")  # Load background bg <- system.file("extdata", "penguins.jpg", package = "overlay")  # Add multiple overlays using the pipe placeholder plotandcode <- hud_overlay(   overlay    = penguin_plot,   background = bg,   placement  = "left",   size       = "xl",   tilt       = "left",   opacity    = 0.85 ) |>    hud_overlay(     overlay    = code_img,     background = _,          # Pipe placeholder - uses result from previous step     placement  = "right",     size       = "xl",     tilt       = "none",     opacity    = 1   )

title: Render and Overlay ggplot2 and gt objects or Code Snippets with Perspective and Transparency
text: function can also called individually, useful debugging fine-grained control intermediate step:
code: library(ggplot2) library(overlay)  # Create a ggplot2 object p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(hp, mpg)) + geom_point()  # Bundled background  bg <- system.file("extdata", "penguins.jpg", package = "overlay")  # Rasterize img <- render_hud(p, width = 500, height = 320, res = 150)  # Add a dark panel with a neon border img <- hud_panel(   img,   padding       = 20,   panel_color   = "#111111CC",   border_color  = "#39FF1488",   border_width  = 8 )  # Perspective warp img <- warp_hud(   img,   corners = list(tl = c(-90, 0), bl = c(-90, 0)) )  # Composite out <- composite_hud(   background = bg,   overlay    = img,   x = 60, y = 80,   opacity    = 0.9 )  magick::image_browse(out)

title: Render and Overlay ggplot2 and gt objects or Code Snippets with Perspective and Transparency
text: addition ggplot2 plots gt tables, syntax-highlighted code snippets can also added using carbon_image(). function uses community-developed carbonara API generate code images styled carbon.js themes. endpoints may always responsive, usually work tries case timeout errors. carbon_image() supports dark light themes default mostly hard-coded, output can customized additional parameters:
code: library(overlay)  # Create a code snippet image plotting_code <- 'library(ggplot2)  ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_point(aes(color = factor(cyl))) +   theme_minimal() +   labs(title = "Fuel Efficiency")'  code_img <- carbon_image(plotting_code, lang = "r", theme = "dark")  # Overlay the code on a background bg <- system.file("extdata", "penguins.jpg", package = "overlay")  out <- bg |>   hud_overlay(code_img,                width = 500,                height = 350,               placement = "center") # Light theme with custom settings code_img <- carbon_image(   plotting_code,   lang = "r",   theme = "light",   fontSize = "16px",   fontFamily = "Fira Code" )

title: Render and Overlay ggplot2 and gt objects or Code Snippets with Perspective and Transparency
text: hud_overlay() composite_hud() return magick-image object, can saved magick::image_write()
code: magick::image_write(out, "result.png")

title: Render and Overlay ggplot2 and gt objects or Code Snippets with Perspective and Transparency
text: magick image processing (requires ImageMagick installed system) ragg crisp high-quality graphics rendering ggplot2 plots using grammar graphics gt great-looking tables httr (suggested) carbon code image API requests

title: Render and Overlay ggplot2 and gt objects or Code Snippets with Perspective and Transparency
text: ImageMagick (required magick). Verify magick::magick_config() R magick -version shell. Fonts: plots/tables use custom fonts, ensure available system.

title: Render and Overlay ggplot2 and gt objects or Code Snippets with Perspective and Transparency
text: package developed extensive input various Large Language models multiple providers (Anthropic, Google, OpenAI, Groq), implemented via autocomplete, Posit Assistant, Positron Assistant, Continue Roo Code extensions Positron, ellmer, Terminal User Interfaces like Gemini CLI. package necessarily ‘vibe-coded’, includes much AI-generated code think ’ll ever include work. Developing overlay part experiment LLM-assisted development, also way get better idea token usage, costs, rate limits, different implementations compare. still take full responsibility code bugs.

title: Render and Overlay ggplot2 and gt objects or Code Snippets with Perspective and Transparency
text: MIT

what: Create an image of a code snippet with carbon.js via the carbonara API — carbon_image
title: Create an image of a code snippet with carbon.js via the carbonara API — carbon_image
text: Generates styled code image using carbonara community API (https://github.com/petersolopov/carbonara), provides programmatic access carbon.js styling without headless browser.

title: Create an image of a code snippet with carbon.js via the carbonara API — carbon_image
code: carbon_image(x, lang = "r", theme = c("dark", "light"), timeout = 60, ...)

title: Create an image of a code snippet with carbon.js via the carbonara API — carbon_image
text: x Character string code render. lang Programming language. Default "r". See carbonara documentation supported languages. theme Overall look. "dark" (default, uses one-dark) "light" (uses one-light). timeout Request timeout seconds. Defaults 60. ... Additional carbonara API parameters. Common options include: backgroundColor: background color (e.g., "rgba(171,184,195,1)") theme: theme name (overrides theme preset, e.g., "monokai", "nord") fontFamily: font family (e.g., "Hack", "Fira Code") fontSize: font size (e.g., "14px") lineNumbers: show line numbers (TRUE FALSE) windowControls: show window controls (TRUE FALSE)

title: Create an image of a code snippet with carbon.js via the carbonara API — carbon_image
text: magick-image object.

title: Create an image of a code snippet with carbon.js via the carbonara API — carbon_image
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Dark theme (default) img <- carbon_image("x <- 1:10\nplot(x)")  # Light theme img <- carbon_image("x <- 1:10\nplot(x)", theme = "light")  # Custom theme and settings img <- carbon_image("x <- 1:10", theme = "dark",                      backgroundColor = "rgba(0,0,0,1)",                     fontSize = "16px") } # }

what: Composite an overlay onto a background image — composite_hud
title: Composite an overlay onto a background image — composite_hud
text: Places magick-image overlay onto background image specified position opacity.

title: Composite an overlay onto a background image — composite_hud
code: composite_hud(   background,   overlay,   x = 0,   y = 0,   opacity = 0.85,   operator = "over",   ... )

title: Composite an overlay onto a background image — composite_hud
text: background path image file, URL, magick-image object use background. overlay magick-image object overlay, typically output render_hud() warp_hud(). x Horizontal pixel offset overlay's top-left corner background's top-left. Defaults 0. y Vertical pixel offset overlay's top-left corner background's top-left. Defaults 0. opacity number [0, 1] controlling overlay's transparency. 1 fully opaque, 0 invisible. Default 0.85. operator ImageMagick compositing operator. Default "". See magick::image_composite() full list operators. ... Additional arguments (currently unused, reserved future extensions).

title: Composite an overlay onto a background image — composite_hud
text: magick-image dimensions background.

title: Composite an overlay onto a background image — composite_hud
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(ggplot2) bg  <- magick::image_read("photo.jpg") p   <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) + geom_point() img <- render_hud(p, width = 400, height = 280) out <- composite_hud(bg, img, x = 50, y = 30, opacity = 0.8) magick::image_browse(out) } # }

what: Render, warp, and composite a ggplot2, gt, or code snippet over a background image — hud_overlay
title: Render, warp, and composite a ggplot2, gt, or code snippet over a background image — hud_overlay
text: convenience wrapper chains render_hud(), (optionally) hud_panel(), (optionally) warp_hud(), composite_hud() single call.

title: Render, warp, and composite a ggplot2, gt, or code snippet over a background image — hud_overlay
code: hud_overlay(   overlay,   background,   x = NULL,   y = NULL,   placement = NULL,   margin = 40L,   size = NULL,   width = NULL,   height = NULL,   panel = FALSE,   tilt = NULL,   corners = NULL,   opacity = 0.85,   bg = "transparent",   res = 150,   keep_size = TRUE,   supersample = 2L,   operator = "over",   ... )

title: Render, warp, and composite a ggplot2, gt, or code snippet over a background image — hud_overlay
text: overlay ggplot2, gt, image code snippet render composite. Placed first ggplot2/gt/code objects can piped directly function. background path image file, URL, magick-image object use background. x Horizontal pixel offset overlay's top-left corner. Overrides placement supplied. Default 0. y Vertical pixel offset overlay's top-left corner. Overrides placement supplied. Default 0. placement Optional placement shorthand. One "left", "right", "centre" ("center"), "top-left", "top-right", "top", "bottom-left", "bottom-right", "bottom". Auto-computes x y background dimensions width/height. Ignored axis x y supplied explicitly. margin Pixel gap overlay background edge placement used. Default 40L. size Optional size preset: "small" (280 × 190), "medium" (420 × 300), "large" (580 × 380), "xl" (760 × 500), "xxl" (960 × 640). Sets width height arguments supplied explicitly. Ignored width height provided. width Width pixels render overlay. Overrides size supplied. Default 400 (size NULL). height Height pixels render overlay. Overrides size supplied. Default 300 (size NULL). panel Controls whether HUD panel frame applied via hud_panel() rendering. Can either: FALSE (default): border. TRUE: apply panel default settings. named list arguments, passed hud_panel() (e.g. list(border_color = "#00FF8866")). tilt Optional tilt preset. One "none", "left", "right", "top", "bottom". "none" applies warp (flat overlay). Generate perspective warp scaled overlay dimensions: "left" / "right" tilt corresponding vertical edge away; "top" / "bottom" recede corresponding horizontal edge. Ignored corners supplied. corners Optional named list corner offset vectors c(dx, dy) passed warp_hud(). Names "tl", "tr", "bl", "br". Overrides tilt supplied. NULL warp applied. opacity Overlay transparency/alpha [0, 1]. Default 0.85. bg Background colour rendered overlay. Default "transparent". res Resolution DPI rasterising overlay. Default 150. keep_size Passed warp_hud() corners NULL. Default TRUE. supersample Integer supersampling factor. Default 2L. overlay rendered panelled supersample times requested pixel dimensions (proportionally scaled DPI panel geometry), warped higher resolution, downscaled Lanczos filter compositing. eliminates horizontal scanline aliasing ImageMagick's perspective distortion produces steep angles. Increase 3L 4L aggressive anti-aliasing. Set 1L disable. operator ImageMagick compositing operator. Default "". ... Additional arguments (currently unused, reserved future extensions).

title: Render, warp, and composite a ggplot2, gt, or code snippet over a background image — hud_overlay
text: magick-image dimensions background.

title: Render, warp, and composite a ggplot2, gt, or code snippet over a background image — hud_overlay
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(ggplot2) library(gt) library(tibble)  # Create a gt table weather_tbl <- tibble(   location = "King George Island, Antarctica",   temp     = "-2 °C",   wind     = "37 km/h" ) |>   gt() |>   tab_header(title = "Weather Report") |>   opt_stylize(style = 6, color = "blue")  # Create a ggplot p <- ggplot(penguins, aes(bill_len, bill_dep)) +   geom_point(color = "#5b9bd5") +   theme_minimal()  # Composite them onto a background bg <- system.file("extdata", "penguins.jpg", package = "overlay")  out <- weather_tbl |>   hud_overlay(     background = bg,     placement  = "top-left",     size       = "medium",     panel      = TRUE,     opacity    = 0.85   ) |>   hud_overlay(     overlay    = p,     background = _,     placement  = "bottom-right",     size       = "medium",     tilt       = "right",     panel      = TRUE,     opacity    = 0.9   )  magick::image_browse(out)  #' # Multiple overlays with pipe placeholder code <- 'ggplot(penguins, aes(bill_len, bill_dep)) + geom_point()' code_img <- carbon_image(code, lang = "r", theme = "dark")  result <- hud_overlay(   overlay = p,   background = bg,   placement = "left",   size = "xl" ) |>   hud_overlay(     overlay = code_img,     background = _,     placement = "right",     size = "xl"   ) } # }

what: Wrap a magick image in a dark semi-transparent panel border — hud_panel
title: Wrap a magick image in a dark semi-transparent panel border — hud_panel
text: Adds dark, rectangular background ("panel") around magick-image, optional colored-border. result larger image original content centred panel, ready warp_hud() composite_hud().

title: Wrap a magick image in a dark semi-transparent panel border — hud_panel
code: hud_panel(   img,   padding = 20,   panel_color = "#111111CC",   border_color = NA,   border_width = 2,   ... )

title: Wrap a magick image in a dark semi-transparent panel border — hud_panel
text: img magick-image object, typically render_hud(). padding Number pixels add around four sides img drawing panel frame. Default 20. panel_color Background colour panel CSS hex string (including alpha channel). Default "#111111CC". border_color Colour border drawn around panel edge. Set NA (default) omit border. border_width Line width border pixels. Defaults 2. ... Additional arguments (currently unused, reserved future extensions).

title: Wrap a magick image in a dark semi-transparent panel border — hud_panel
text: magick-image size width + 2*padding height + 2*padding relative input.

title: Wrap a magick image in a dark semi-transparent panel border — hud_panel
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(ggplot2) p   <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) + geom_point() img <- render_hud(p, width = 480, height = 320, bg = "transparent")  # panel with a green border panel <- hud_panel(img,                    panel_color  = "#1B6B3ACC",                    border_color = "#57FF8877",                    border_width = 2) } # }

what: overlay: Render and Overlay ggplot2 and gt objects or Code Snippets with Perspective and Transparency — overlay-package
title: overlay: Render and Overlay ggplot2 and gt objects or Code Snippets with Perspective and Transparency — overlay-package
text: Renders ggplot2 plots, gt tables, carbon.js code images magick objects, applies optional perspective warp distortions, composites background images transparency.

title: overlay: Render and Overlay ggplot2 and gt objects or Code Snippets with Perspective and Transparency — overlay-package
text: Maintainer: Luis D. Verde Arregoitia luis@liomys.mx (ORCID) [copyright holder]

what: Render a ggplot2, gt, or carbon code image to a magick image — render_hud
title: Render a ggplot2, gt, or carbon code image to a magick image — render_hud
text: Converts ggplot2 plot, gt table, carbon.js code image magick-image, ready warping compositing. Dispatches automatically based class x.

title: Render a ggplot2, gt, or carbon code image to a magick image — render_hud
code: render_hud(x, width = 400, height = 300, bg = "transparent", res = 150, ...)

title: Render a ggplot2, gt, or carbon code image to a magick image — render_hud
text: x ggplot2 ("ggplot"), gt ("gt_tbl"), magick image object (carbon_image()). width Width output image pixels. Default 400. height Height output image pixels. Default 300. bg Background color passed graphics device. Use "transparent" (default) keep alpha compositing. Note: gt objects rendered via gt::gtsave() may fully honor transparent background; case use dark colour "#00000000" accept white panel, style gt table background directly. carbon_image() objects argument ignored carbon.js controls background via bg_color. res Resolution DPI used rasterizing plot. Default 150. Ignored carbon_image() objects. ... Additional arguments, currently unused reserved future extensions.

title: Render a ggplot2, gt, or carbon code image to a magick image — render_hud
text: magick-image object.

title: Render a ggplot2, gt, or carbon code image to a magick image — render_hud
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(ggplot2) p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) + geom_point() img <- render_hud(p, width = 500, height = 350)  # Carbon code block code_img <- carbon_image('fit <- lm(mpg ~ wt, data = mtcars)\\nsummary(fit)') img <- render_hud(code_img, width = 600, height = 300) } # }

what: Apply a perspective warp to a magick image — warp_hud
title: Apply a perspective warp to a magick image — warp_hud
text: Distorts magick-image using four-point perspective transformation, giving "floating HUD panel" effect.

title: Apply a perspective warp to a magick image — warp_hud
code: warp_hud(img, corners = list(), keep_size = TRUE, ...)

title: Apply a perspective warp to a magick image — warp_hud
text: img magick-image object, typically produced render_hud(). corners named list specifying far corner image move natural position. element numeric vector length 2: c(dx, dy), positive values move right/. Names must "tl" (top-left), "tr" (top-right), "bl" (bottom-left), "br" (bottom-right). corner omitted defaults c(0, 0) (movement). Example: tilt panel left edge rises right edge drops:   keep_size TRUE (default), output image cropped/padded back original pixel dimensions. FALSE, ImageMagick may expand canvas contain warped result. ... Currently unused.

title: Apply a perspective warp to a magick image — warp_hud
text: warped magick-image alpha channel preserved.

title: Apply a perspective warp to a magick image — warp_hud
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(ggplot2) p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) + geom_point() img <- render_hud(p, width = 500, height = 350)  # Tilt the top edge backward (raise both top corners) warped <- warp_hud(img, corners = list(tl = c(20, -15), tr = c(-20, -15))) magick::image_browse(warped) } # }
